sprint: Sprint 3 follow-up — Result screen overflow fix
task: Reported with a tall/portrait photo of a physical canvas painting: the Result screen's before/after stage sized itself from CSS aspect-ratio + max-width alone, which clamps width but not height, so a portrait photo could grow the stage past the viewport and force the whole page to scroll. Replaced with an explicit JS sizing pass (sizeBeforeAfterStage) that fits the stage within both an available width and an available height computed from the page's actual measured content height (a first attempt using a fixed fraction of the viewport measured wrong on shorter windows). Caught and fixed a real bug in the same pass: the fix initially referenced els.beforeAfter, never added to the els lookup object, throwing and silently falling back to the old broken sizing -- found via a direct portrait-photo regression test. Verified via Playwright across several image shapes and viewport sizes, including an 8-run stress test on a case that first looked inconsistent (confirmed as transient test-environment noise, reproducibly correct afterward): the stage is always the largest size that fits with zero page scroll. Full existing regression suite unchanged.
